testcases.input: Employee table: +----+-------+--------+--------------+ | id | name | salary | departmentId | +----+-------+--------+--------------+ | 1 | Joe | 70000 | 1 | | 2 | Jim | 90000 | 1 | | 3 | Henry | 80000 | 2 | | 4 | Sam | 60000 | 2 | | 5 | Max | 90000 | 1 | +----+-------+--------+--------------+ Department table: +----+-------+ | id | name | +----+-------+ | 1 | IT | | 2 | Sales | +----+-------+ Output: +------------+----------+--------+ | Department | Employee | Salary | +------------+----------+--------+ | IT | Jim | 90000 | | Sales | Henry | 80000 | | IT | Max | 90000 | +------------+----------+--------+ Explanation: Max and Jim both have the highest salary in the IT department and Henry has the highest salary in the Sales department.
